docs.title: Django ERD Generator
docs.text: Django ERD Generator is a comprehensive command-line tool designed to generate Entity-Relationship Diagrams (ERDs) and data dictionaries from Django models. It supports multiple output formats, making it easy to visualise database relationships in different diagramming tools and create detailed documentation.

docs.title: Quick Overview
docs.text:
• ERD Generation: Create visual database diagrams in multiple formats
  • Data Dictionary: Generate comprehensive model documentation in Markdown
  • Multiple Dialects: Support for Mermaid.js, PlantUML, and dbdiagram.io
  • Flexible Output: Console output or file export
  • App Filtering: Include specific Django apps or all apps
  • Rich Documentation: Field types, constraints, relationships, and help text

docs.title: Supported ERD Dialects
docs.text:
• Mermaid.js
  • PlantUML
  • dbdiagram.io

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
pip install django-erd-generator

Add 'django_erd_generator' to your INSTALLED_APPS in Django settings.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
Generate an ERD in Mermaid format:

python manage.py generate_erd -d mermaid

Generate a data dictionary:

python manage.py generate_data_dictionary -o docs/data_dictionary.md

docs.title: Why Use Django ERD Generator?
docs.text:
Keeping database schema documentation up to date is challenging. This tool automates the process by:

  1. Auto-generating diagrams from your Django models
  2. Creating data dictionaries with field details and relationships
  3. Supporting multiple formats for different tools and platforms
  4. Staying in sync with your codebase - just regenerate when models change

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Installation Guide - Detailed setup instructions
  • Quickstart Guide - Get started in 5 minutes
  • Features Overview - Explore all capabilities

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text: Command-line interface reference for Django ERD Generator.

docs.title: generate_erd
docs.text: Generate Entity-Relationship Diagram from Django models.

docs.title: Command
docs.text: python manage.py generate_erd [options]

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Type Default Description -h, --help flag - Show help message -a, --apps string All apps Comma-separated list of apps to include -d, --dialect string mermaid Output format: mermaid, plantuml, dbdiagram -o, --output string Console Output file path

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Generate Mermaid ERD to console
python manage.py generate_erd -d mermaid

# Generate PlantUML ERD to file
python manage.py generate_erd -d plantuml -o erd.puml

# Generate ERD for specific apps
python manage.py generate_erd -a auth,shop -d dbdiagram

docs.title: generate_data_dictionary
docs.text: Generate Markdown data dictionary from Django models.

docs.title: Command
docs.text: python manage.py generate_data_dictionary [options]

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Type Default Description -h, --help flag - Show help message -a, --apps string All apps Comma-separated list of apps to include -o, --output string Console Output file path

docs.title: Examples
docs.text:
# Generate data dictionary to console
python manage.py generate_data_dictionary

# Generate data dictionary to file
python manage.py generate_data_dictionary -o docs/data_dictionary.md

# Generate for specific apps only
python manage.py generate_data_dictionary -a shop -o docs/shop_dictionary.md

docs.title: Exit Codes
docs.text: Code Meaning 0 Success 1 Error (missing models, invalid options, etc.)

docs.title: Errors
docs.text:
Common errors and solutions:

  • Models not found: Check app names in -a parameter
  • Dialect not supported: Use mermaid, plantuml, or dbdiagram
  • Database not configured: Run python manage.py migrate first

docs.title: Changelog

docs.title: Added

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text: Thanks for your interest in contributing to Django ERD Generator!

docs.title: Development Setup
docs.text:
1. Fork and clone the repository 

    git clone https://github.com/your-username/django-erd.git
cd django-erd

  2. Install dependencies 

    uv sync --dev

  3. Set up pre-commit hooks 

    pre-commit install

docs.title: Development Workflow
docs.text:
1. Create a feature branch: 

    git checkout -b feature/your-feature

  2. Make your changes

  3. Run tests: 

    pytest

  4. Run linter and formatter: 

    ruff check .
ruff format .

  5. Commit your changes: 

    git commit -m "Add your feature"

  6. Push and create a pull request

docs.title: Code Style
docs.text:
• Formatting: ruff (line length: 88)
  • Linting: ruff (E, F, W, I, UP, B, C4, SIM)
  • Type hints: Preferred but not required

docs.title: Testing
docs.text:
• Write tests for new features
  • Maintain test coverage
  • Run pytest before submitting PR

docs.title: Pull Request Guidelines
docs.text:
1. Clear title and description
  2. Link related issues
  3. Update documentation if needed
  4. Ensure all tests pass
  5. Add changelog entry

docs.title: Questions?
docs.text: Open an issue for questions or discussion.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.9+
  • Django 4.2+

docs.title: Install via pip
docs.text: pip install django-erd-generator

docs.title: Add to Django Project
docs.text:
Add 'django_erd_generator' to your INSTALLED_APPS in settings.py:

INSTALLED_APPS = [
    # ...
    "django_erd_generator",
]

docs.title: Development Installation
docs.text:
For contributing or running tests:

# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/marzukia/django-erd.git
cd django-erd

# Install uv (if not already installed)
curl -LsSf https://astral.sh/uv/install.sh | sh

# Create virtual environment
uv venv
source .venv/bin/activate  # On Unix/macOS
# or
.venv\Scripts\activate  # On Windows

# Install dependencies
uv sync --dev

docs.title: Verify Installation
docs.text:
Run the help command to verify installation:

python manage.py generate_erd --help
python manage.py generate_data_dictionary --help

docs.title: Optional: GIS Support
docs.text:
If you want to use GIS fields (PointField, PolygonField, etc.), make sure you have GeoDjango set up:

INSTALLED_APPS = [
    # ...
    "django.contrib.gis",
    "django_erd_generator",
]

See GIS Fields for more details.

docs.title: Quickstart
docs.text: Get started with Django ERD Generator in 5 minutes.

docs.title: 1. Basic ERD Generation
docs.text:
Generate an ERD for all apps in Mermaid format:

python manage.py generate_erd -d mermaid

docs.title: 2. Save to File
docs.text:
Save the ERD to a file:

python manage.py generate_erd -d mermaid -o erd.md

docs.title: 3. Filter by Apps
docs.text:
Generate ERD for specific apps only:

python manage.py generate_erd -a shopping,polls -d mermaid

docs.title: 4. Different Formats
docs.text:
Mermaid.js: 

python manage.py generate_erd -d mermaid -o erd.md

PlantUML: 

python manage.py generate_erd -d plantuml -o erd.puml

dbdiagram.io: 

python manage.py generate_erd -d dbdiagram -o erd.txt

docs.title: Generate Data Dictionary
docs.text:
Create comprehensive documentation:

python manage.py generate_data_dictionary -o docs/data_dictionary.md

docs.title: Mermaid.js Live Editor
docs.text:
1. Generate ERD: python manage.py generate_erd -d mermaid -o erd.md
  2. Copy the output
  3. Paste into Mermaid Live Editor

docs.title: PlantUML Server
docs.text:
1. Generate ERD: python manage.py generate_erd -d plantuml -o erd.puml
  2. Upload to PlantUML Server

docs.title: dbdiagram.io
docs.text:
1. Generate ERD: python manage.py generate_erd -d dbdiagram -o erd.txt
  2. Import into dbdiagram.io

docs.title: Sample Model
docs.text:
from django.db import models

class Customer(models.Model):
    first_name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    last_name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    email = models.EmailField(unique=True)

class Order(models.Model):
    customer = models.ForeignKey(Customer, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    order_date = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    total = models.DecimalField(max_digits=10, decimal_places=2)

docs.title: Generated Mermaid ERD
docs.text:
erDiagram
    Customer {
        varchar first_name
        varchar last_name
        varchar email
    }
    Order {
        ForeignKey customer
        datetime order_date
        decimal total
    }
    Customer ||--o{ Order : places

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Features - Explore all capabilities
  • Supported Dialects - Compare output formats
  • Data Dictionary - Generate detailed documentation

docs.title: Data Dictionary
docs.text: Generate comprehensive Markdown documentation from your Django models.

docs.title: Basic Usage
docs.text: python manage.py generate_data_dictionary -o docs/data_dictionary.md

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Description Example -a APPS Filter by specific apps -a shopping,polls -o OUTPUT Output file path -o docs/dictionary.md

docs.title: Output Structure
docs.text:
The data dictionary includes:

  • Model Overview: Description and database table name
  • Field Details: Type, constraints, nullability
  • Relationships: Foreign keys, many-to-many relationships
  • Help Text: Documentation from field definitions
  • Meta Options: Ordering, verbose names, unique_together

docs.title: Example Output
docs.text:
# Data Dictionary

## Customer

**Table:** `auth_customer`

### Fields

| Field | Type | Required | Unique | Description |
|-------|------|----------|--------|-------------|
| id | bigint | Yes | Yes | Auto-incrementing primary key |
| first_name | varchar(100) | Yes | No | Customer's first name |
| last_name | varchar(100) | Yes | No | Customer's last name |
| email | varchar(254) | Yes | Yes | Email address (unique) |

### Relationships

- **Orders**: One-to-many relationship with Order model

## Order

**Table:** `sales_order`

### Fields

| Field | Type | Required | Unique | Description |
|-------|------|----------|--------|-------------|
| id | bigint | Yes | Yes | Auto-incrementing primary key |
| customer | bigint | Yes | No | Reference to Customer |
| order_date | datetime | Yes | No | Date and time of order |
| total | decimal(10,2) | Yes | No | Order total amount |

### Relationships

- **Customer**: Many-to-one relationship to Customer model

docs.title: MkDocs
docs.text:
1. Generate dictionary: 

    python manage.py generate_data_dictionary -o docs/data_dictionary.md

  2. Add to mkdocs.yml: 

    nav:
  - Data Dictionary: data_dictionary.md

docs.title: GitHub Wiki
docs.text:
1. Generate dictionary
  2. Copy content to GitHub Wiki pages
  3. Update on each model change

docs.title: Best Practices
docs.text:
• Generate data dictionary alongside ERDs
  • Include in API documentation
  • Update on every model change (add to CI/CD)
  • Filter by app for modular documentation

docs.title: Comparison: ERD vs Data Dictionary
docs.text: Aspect ERD Data Dictionary Format Visual diagram Structured text Best For Quick overview Detailed reference Content Models, relationships Field properties, constraints Use Case Design reviews Technical documentation

docs.title: ERD Generation
docs.text: Generate Entity-Relationship Diagrams from your Django models.

docs.title: Basic Usage
docs.text: python manage.py generate_erd -d mermaid

docs.title: Options
docs.text: Option Description Example -a APPS Filter by specific apps -a shopping,polls -d DIALECT Output format -d mermaid, -d plantuml, -d dbdiagram -o OUTPUT Output file path -o erd.md

docs.title: Mermaid.js
docs.text:
Best for: GitHub README files, documentation sites

python manage.py generate_erd -d mermaid -o erd.md

Output: 

erDiagram
    Customer {
        varchar first_name
        varchar last_name
        varchar email
    }
    Order {
        ForeignKey customer
        datetime order_date
        decimal total
    }
    Customer ||--o{ Order : places

docs.title: PlantUML
docs.text:
Best for: Detailed diagrams, enterprise documentation

python manage.py generate_erd -d plantuml -o erd.puml

Output: 

@startuml
entity Customer {
    * id : bigint
    --
    varchar first_name
    varchar last_name
    varchar email
}
entity Order {
    * id : bigint
    --
    ForeignKey customer
    datetime order_date
    decimal total
}
Customer ||--o{ Order : places
@enduml

docs.title: dbdiagram.io
docs.text:
Best for: Database design, quick prototyping

python manage.py generate_erd -d dbdiagram -o erd.txt

Output: 

Table Customer {
  id bigint [pk]
  first_name varchar
  last_name varchar
  email varchar
}

Table Order {
  id bigint [pk]
  customer bigint [ref: > Customer.id]
  order_date datetime
  total decimal
}

Ref: Order.customer > Customer.id

docs.title: App Filtering
docs.text:
Include only specific apps:

python manage.py generate_erd -a auth,shopping -d mermaid

Exclude apps by not listing them in the -a parameter.

docs.title: GitHub README
docs.text:
1. Generate ERD: 

    python manage.py generate_erd -d mermaid -o docs/erd.md

  2. Add to README: 

    ## Database Schema

```mermaid
```mermaid
# Paste content from erd.md
    ```

docs.title: MkDocs Integration
docs.text:
Add to your docs folder and reference in mkdocs.yml:

nav:
  - Database Schema: erd.md

docs.title: Tips
docs.text:
• Use -d mermaid for GitHub-compatible diagrams
  • Use -d plantuml for complex schemas
  • Use -d dbdiagram for database design collaboration
  • Save output to files for version control
  • Regenerate ERDs after model changes

docs.title: GIS Fields Support
docs.text: Django ERD Generator supports GeoDjango GIS field types for spatial data modeling.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
Enable GIS support in your Django project:

# settings.py
INSTALLED_APPS = [
    # ...
    "django.contrib.gis",
    "django_erd_generator",
]

docs.title: Supported GIS Fields
docs.text: Field Type Description Mermaid Type PlantUML Type PointField Point coordinates geometry_point POINT LineStringField Line/path geometries geometry_linestring LINESTRING PolygonField Area/region geometries geometry_polygon POLYGON MultiPointField Collection of points geometry_multipoint MULTIPOINT MultiLineStringField Collection of lines geometry_multilinestring MULTILINESTRING MultiPolygonField Collection of polygons geometry_multipolygon MULTIPOLYGON GeometryCollectionField Mixed geometries geometry_collection GEOMETRYCOLLECTION GeometryField Generic geometry geometry GEOMETRY RasterField Raster/image data geometry_raster RASTER

docs.title: Example Model
docs.text:
from django.contrib.gis.db import models

class Location(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    point = models.PointField()
    boundary = models.PolygonField()
    routes = models.MultiLineStringField()
    metadata = models.GeometryField()  # Generic geometry

class Store(models.Model):
    name = models.CharField(max_length=100)
    location = models.PointField()
    delivery_zone = models.PolygonField()

docs.title: Mermaid.js
docs.text:
erDiagram
    Location {
        varchar name
        geometry_point point
        geometry_polygon boundary
        geometry_multilinestring routes
        geometry metadata
    }
    Store {
        varchar name
        geometry_point location
        geometry_polygon delivery_zone
    }

docs.title: PlantUML
docs.text:
@startuml
entity Location {
    * id : bigint
    --
    varchar name
    POINT point
    POLYGON boundary
    MULTILINESTRING routes
    GEOMETRY metadata
}
entity Store {
    * id : bigint
    --
    varchar name
    POINT location
    POLYGON delivery_zone
}
@enduml

docs.title: Use Cases
docs.text:
• Mapping applications: Store locations, boundaries, routes
  • Geofencing: Define delivery zones, service areas
  • Spatial analysis: Geographic relationships and queries
  • Real estate: Property boundaries, lot sizes
  • Logistics: Route planning, delivery zones

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Django with GeoDjango support
  • PostGIS database (for production)
  • GEOS and GDAL libraries installed

docs.title: Notes
docs.text:
• GIS fields require a spatial database backend
  • Use from django.contrib.gis.db import models instead of from django.db import models
  • All standard Django field types work alongside GIS fields

docs.title: Supported Dialects
docs.text: Compare the three supported ERD output formats.

docs.title: Mermaid.js
docs.text: Best for: GitHub READMEs, web documentation, quick sharing

docs.title: Pros
docs.text:
• Native GitHub support
  • No external tools needed
  • Easy to edit in markdown
  • Live preview in browsers

docs.title: Cons
docs.text:
• Limited styling options
  • Simpler diagram syntax

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
erDiagram
    Customer {
        varchar first_name
        varchar last_name
        varchar email
    }
    Order {
        ForeignKey customer
        datetime order_date
    }
    Customer ||--o{ Order : places

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: python manage.py generate_erd -d mermaid -o erd.md

docs.title: PlantUML
docs.text: Best for: Enterprise documentation, detailed diagrams, printing

docs.title: Pros
docs.text:
• Highly customizable
  • Professional output
  • Many export formats (PNG, SVG, PDF)
  • Rich relationship notation

docs.title: Cons
docs.text:
• Requires PlantUML server or local installation
  • Steeper learning curve

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
@startuml
entity Customer {
    * id : bigint
    --
    varchar first_name
    varchar last_name
    varchar email
}
entity Order {
    * id : bigint
    --
    ForeignKey customer
    datetime order_date
}
Customer ||--o{ Order : places
@enduml

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: python manage.py generate_erd -d plantuml -o erd.puml

docs.title: dbdiagram.io
docs.text: Best for: Database design, collaboration, SQL generation

docs.title: Pros
docs.text:
• Interactive web editor
  • SQL export capability
  • Team collaboration
  • Database schema validation

docs.title: Cons
docs.text:
• Requires internet connection for web tool
  • Proprietary platform

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
Table Customer {
  id bigint [pk]
  first_name varchar
  last_name varchar
  email varchar [unique]
}

Table Order {
  id bigint [pk]
  customer bigint [ref: > Customer.id]
  order_date datetime
}

Ref: Order.customer > Customer.id

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: python manage.py generate_erd -d dbdiagram -o erd.txt

docs.title: Comparison Table
docs.text: Feature Mermaid.js PlantUML dbdiagram.io GitHub Support ✅ Native ❌ Image only ❌ Image only Customization Limited High Medium Export Formats SVG/PNG PNG/SVG/PDF PNG/SQL Learning Curve Low Medium Low Offline Use ✅ Yes ✅ Yes ❌ No Team Collaboration ❌ No ❌ No ✅ Yes SQL Generation ❌ No ❌ No ✅ Yes

docs.title: Recommendation
docs.text:
• Use Mermaid.js for GitHub projects and README files
  • Use PlantUML for enterprise documentation and printing
  • Use dbdiagram.io for database design and team collaboration
